title: Natural fibers having absorbable and hydrophobic properties for making surgical sutures and surgical meshes
abstract: Suture materials for producing medical devices are provided. The suture materials can include natural fibers, and can be suitable for making multifilament sutures and surgical meshes comprising multifilament sutures as examples of medical devices. The multifilament suture can include a plurality of filaments combined together according to a twined pattern, and at least one filament of the plurality of filaments can include natural fibers, synthetic fibers, or mineral-based fibers. A suture device can include the multifilament suture combined with a suture needle. The multifilament suture can be coated with a biocompatible coating, such as a bioactive agent, and/or include a first set of filaments that includes natural fibers and a second set of filaments that includes natural fibers that are different from the natural fibers of the first set of filaments, synthetic fibers or mineral-based fibers, for instance.
claims:
Claims 1 . A multifilament suture for use as a surgical suture, the multifilament suture comprising: a plurality of filaments combined together according to a twined pattern, at least one filament of the plurality of filaments comprising natural fibers, the natural fibers being both absorbable and hydrophobic at physiological conditions. 2. The multifilament suture of claim 1 , wherein the natural fibers of the at least one filament are absorbable by proteolysis and/or hydrolysis. 3. The multifilament suture of claim 1 or 2, wherein the at least one filament of the plurality of filaments is configured to prevent bacterial adhesion in an interstice defined between adjacent filaments of the plurality of filaments. 4. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the natural fibers comprise cellulosic plant fibers. 5. The multifilament suture of claim 4, wherein the cellulosic plant fibers comprise milkweed fibers. 6. The multifilament suture of claim 5, wherein the milkweed fibers include one or more of Asclepias Syriaca fibers and Asclepias Speciosa fibers. 7. The multifilament suture of claim 5 or 6, wherein the plurality of filaments further comprises additional natural fibers that are different from the milkweed fibers. 8. The multifilament suture of claim 7, wherein the additional natural fibers comprise cellulosic plant fibers. 9. The multifilament suture of claim 7 or 8, wherein the additional natural fibers comprise one or more of kapok fibers, bamboo fibers, linen fibers, cotton fibers, abaca fibers, acetate fibers, banana fibers, coir fibers, flax fibers, hemp fibers, jute fibers, kenaf fibers, lyocell fibers, modal fibers, pina fibers, raffia fibers, ramie fibers, rayon fibers, sisal fibers, and soy protein fibers. 10. The multifilament suture of claim 7, wherein the additional natural fibers comprise animal fibers. 11 . The multifilament suture of claim 7 or 10, wherein the additional natural fibers comprise one or more of spider silk fibers, wool fibers, alpaca fibers, angora wool fibers, azlon fibers, byssus fibers, camel hair fibers, cashmere wool fibers, chiengora fibers, 

 lambswool fibers, llama fibers, mohair wool fibers, qiviut fibers, rabbit fibers, silk fibers, eri silk fibers, vicuna fibers, and yak fibers. 12. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the plurality of filaments further comprises synthetic fibers. 13. The multifilament suture of claim 12, wherein the synthetic fibres comprise one or more of acrylic fibers, Kevlarâ¢ fibers, modacrylic fibers, Nomexâ¢ fibers, nylon fibers, polyester fibers, spandex fibers, and rayon fibers. 14. The multifilament suture of claim 12, wherein the synthetic fibres are absorbable synthetic fibers. 15. The multifilament suture of claim 14, wherein the absorbable synthetic fibers comprise one or more of polyglactin, lactides, polyglycolic acid, polylactic acid, glycolide, p- dioxanone, epsilon-caprolactone, and trimethylene carbonate. 16. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the plurality of filaments further comprises mineral-based fibers. 17. The multifilament suture of claim 16, wherein the mineral-based fibers comprise one or more of glass fibers, and fiberglass fibers. 18. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the filaments of the plurality of filaments are braided or interlaced according to the twined pattern. 19. The multifilament suture of claim 18, wherein the twined pattern is a one over one twined pattern. 20. The multifilament suture of claim 18, wherein the twined pattern is a one over two twined pattern. 21. The multifilament suture of claim 18, wherein the twined pattern is a two over two twined pattern. 22. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the twined pattern defines a core and a braided sheath provided around the core. 23. The multifilament suture of claim 22, wherein the filaments of the plurality of filaments are combined together to form yarns, and the braided sheath comprises braided yarns. 24. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 23, further comprising a biocompatible coating provided onto an external surface of the multifilament suture. 25. The multifilament suture of claim 24, wherein the biocompatible coating is configured to reduce tissue-drag of the multifilament suture. 26. The multifilament suture of claim 24 or 25, wherein a thickness of the biocompatible coating is at least a quarter of an average diameter of the filaments of the plurality of filaments. 27. The multifilament suture of claim 24 or 25, wherein the thickness of the biocompatible coating is at least a half of the average diameter of the filaments of the plurality of filaments. 28. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 24 to 27, wherein the biocompatible coating comprises an absorbable polymer. 29. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 24 to 28, wherein the biocompatible coating comprises a bioactive agent having therapeutic properties. 30. The multifilament suture of claim 29, wherein the bioactive agent comprises one or more of a cell growth promoter, a cell growth inhibitor, an antibiotic, a cytokine, a healing promoter, a clotting modulator, an anti-inflammatory, and an anti-scarring agent. 31. The multifilament suture of any one of claims 1 to 30, wherein the multifilament suture has a diameter between about 0.01 mm and about 0.7 mm. 32. The multifilament suture of claim 31 , wherein the multifilament suture has a diameter between about 0.1 mm and about 0.5 mm. 33. A surgical mesh for implantation at a surgical site, the surgical mesh comprising: a plurality of multifilament sutures each comprising filaments, the plurality of multifilament sutures being combined together according to a mesh pattern, at least one filament of the plurality of multifilament sutures comprising natural fibers, the natural fibers being both absorbable and hydrophobic at physiological conditions. 34.
